display_name: Men’s Wellness & Hormone Optimization Intake Classifier
purpose: Classify male wellness goals and symptom domains without assuming testosterone therapy candidacy.
Map total T, free T, SHBG, LH, FSH, estradiol, DHEA-S, thyroid, metabolic, and safety labs into wellness interpretation.
Allow wellness goals to trigger evaluation while requiring medical eligibility before testosterone is considered.
rule: Testosterone may be considered only after provider review of symptoms/signs, repeat morning testosterone pattern, fertility goals, hematocrit, prostate/PSA context, BP/CV risk, OSA/LUTS status, and monitoring readiness.

purpose: Route men desiring fertility away from automatic exogenous testosterone and toward fertility-preserving physician review.
Screen prostate cancer history, PSA/prostate findings, metastatic disease, and severe LUTS/BPH before testosterone pathway.
Require baseline and follow-up hematocrit logic before and during testosterone therapy.
Screen BP, heart failure, recent MI/stroke, thrombophilia/VTE risk, and unstable CV symptoms; include 2025 FDA BP warning and TRAVERSE CV context.
Screen untreated severe OSA and severe LUTS before testosterone pathway.
Provide non-testosterone wellness interventions for sleep, training, nutrition, metabolic risk, supplements, weight/waist, alcohol, and medication contributors.
Track symptom response, sexual function, energy, body composition, training recovery, adverse effects, hematocrit, BP, PSA/prostate context, and continuation/discontinuation logic.
